understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to maneuver a selectable piece (and potentially switch between multiple selectables) around the grid within a limited step budget to reach the goal tile(s) (marked with '+'), likely by positioning a piece on the goal to clear the level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Each MOVE action shifted the currently selected piece by one cell, which corresponded to 3 pixels in the coordinate system (so positions changed by ±3 on x or y). Some adjacent objects could be pushable blocks that would move along if contacted in the movement direction, while walls/boundaries could block or otherwise constrain movement in non-uniform ways (the blocked flags were only hints and not guarantees). If more than one selectable piece existed, control could be swapped using CLICK on the other piece’s exact pixel/grid position shown in the state (not semantic-grid indices).
